back_translation: On the third day there was a wedding at the town of Cana in the region of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
Jesus and his disciples were also invited to that wedding.
When the wine ran out, the mother of Jesus said to him, "They have no wine left."
Jesus answered his mother, "Woman, what does this matter have to do with us and you? My hour has not yet come."
His mother said to the servants, "Whatever he tells you, do that."
There were six stone water jars there, set out for the purification according to Jewish custom; each one held about two or three metretes (approximately 80 to 120 liters) of water.
Jesus said to them, "Fill these jars completely with water," and they filled them up to the mouth/brim of the jar.
He said to them, "Now draw some out and take it to the supervisor of the feast." So they took it to him.

When the supervisor of the feast had tasted the water that had become wine, he did not know where it came from, but the servants who had drawn the water knew. The supervisor of the feast then called the bridegroom.
And he said to him, "Everyone brings out the fine wine first, and when the guests are drunk, then brings out the inferior wine. But you have kept the fine wine until now."
Jesus performed this sign as the beginning of all the signs at Cana in Galilee, and he revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
After this event he went down to Capernaum, together with his mother, his younger brothers, and his disciples, and they stayed there for a few days.
At that time the Passover festival of the Jews was drawing near, so Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
In the temple precincts he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and money changers sitting there.

He took some cords and made a whip, and drove everyone out of the temple precincts, both the flock of sheep and the herd of cattle. He poured out the silver coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
He said to those selling doves, "Take these things out of here. Do not make my Father's house a place of trade."
His disciples remembered that it is written, "Zeal for your house will consume me."
Because of this event, the Jews answered and said to him, "What sign will you show us, that you do these things?"
Jesus answered them, "Destroy this sanctuary, and in three days I will raise it up again."
The Jews said, "This sanctuary has been built for forty-six years, and you will raise it up in three days?"
But he was speaking of the sanctuary of his own body.
Therefore, when he had risen from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had spoken thus, and they believed the Scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
When he was staying in Jerusalem during that Passover festival, many people believed in his name because they saw the signs that he was performing.
But Jesus himself did not entrust himself to them, because he knew everyone.
And he did not need anyone to bear witness about humankind, for he himself knew what was in the heart of humankind.
